channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Rheinmetall sells struggling auto division to focus on defense | Defense News
item.description: The unit will be sold to Aequita for a provisional €350 million, subject to potential adjustment when the deal closes, expected in the fourth quarter.

item.title: 🏴 Vladimir Putin’s Second-Biggest Headache | Foreign Policy
item.description: With his war in Ukraine going badly, he may soon face another quagmire in Chechnya.

item.title: 📍 The promise and limits of the new G20 template for debt restructuring | Atlantic Council
item.description: A new G20 roadmap aims to streamline sovereign debt restructurings. Yet by reducing flexibility for creditors and debtors alike, it could make future debt crises harder to resolve. The post The promise and limits of the new G20 template for debt restructuring appeared first on…

item.title: 🌏 Forged in a Knife Fight: China’s Brutal Domestic AI Competition | War on the Rocks
item.description: China’s plan to become a world leader in AI by 2030 is a fixture of practically every Congressional briefing and expert commentary on Beijing’s AI ambitions. The plan’s logic — introduced in 2017 — was simple and alarming: Beijing would direct capital, mobilize its firms,…

item.title: 🌏 Revisiting The Importance of the Battle of Midway | War on the Rocks
item.description: The Battle of Midway has assumed a place in American naval lore that has put it on par with other great battles in world naval history. What Salamis was for the Greeks, Trafalgar for the British Royal Navy, and Tsushima for the Japanese, the clash northwest of Midway Island on…

item.title: 🌍 Wrong Audience, Wrong Ask: Why Trump’s Abraham Accords Gambit Falls on Deaf Ears | War on the Rocks
item.description: When President Donald Trump repeatedly pressed regional leaders on Abraham Accords expansion late last month — framing Arab-Israeli normalization as a debt owed and a condition for a settlement to end the Iran war — he apparently commented there had been silence on the other end…

item.title: 🌏 The missile gap problem: US Indo-Pacific deterrence under strain | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The US commitment to Indo-Pacific stability is being tested not by strategic ambiguity but by industrial reality. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth’s recent notification to Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi that deliveries of US-made Tomahawk ...

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The National Interest: Iran’s Mafia State Understands Only Law Backed by Power | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The National Interest: Iran’s Mafia State Understands Only Law Backed by Power appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 The Threat of Unrest Is Decarbonizing the Global South | Foreign Policy
item.description: Oil and gas price spikes are doing what decades of climate diplomacy struggled to achieve.

item.title: 🏴 The Transatlantic Crucible | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The crisis between Washington and Europe may be a blessing in disguise.

item.title: 🏛️ The American Military’s Coming Marathon | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The Pentagon needs both quantity and quality to win modern conflicts.

item.title: 📍 Can ASEAN’s Green Goals Survive Its Data Center Boom? | The Diplomat
item.description: Data centers are critical enablers of ASEAN’s digital economy, but rapid digitalization presents a number of serious sustainability challenges.

item.title: 📍 To Lam’s Southeast Asia Visits Expand Vietnam’s ‘Neighborhood Bamboo Diplomacy’ | The Diplomat
item.description: Hanoi's closer engagement with its ASEAN neighbors represents an enhancement of its flexible, omnidirectional foreign policy doctrine.

item.title: 🏛️ Lawmakers demand answers about $620M Pentagon loan to firm tied to Trump Jr. | Defense One
item.description: In a letter, a group of Democratic Congress members wrote that ProPublica’s reporting on the deal “reveals a staggering level of corruption and influence.”

item.title: 🛡️ Can ‘Compulsory Conciliation’ Resolve the Cambodia-Thailand Maritime Border Dispute? | The Diplomat
item.description: Bangkok's recent withdrawal from a 2001 MoU governing maritime border talks has prompted Phnom Penh to internationalize the dispute.

item.title: 🔐 Australia’s cybersecurity workforce problem: language that repels the people we need | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia has a cybersecurity workforce problem, and part of the explanation is hiding in plain sight: the language of the field actively repels the people we need. Last year, I was on a panel for ...

item.title: 📍 Major ASEAN Economies In Line For New US Tariffs Over Forced Labor | The Diplomat
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has recommended the imposition 10-12.5 percent levies on 60 major trading partners.

item.title: 🛡️ The Afghanistan-Pakistan Border Is Still Unstable | Foreign Policy
item.description: There is a brief lull in violence, but the root causes of the conflict haven’t been addressed.

item.title: 🕵️ The Acting DNI and the Intelligence Office Trump Wants | Just Security
item.description: Bill Pulte’s appointment as Acting Director of National Intelligence suggests that ODNI may now be serving a more political function than advising the president. The post The Acting DNI and the Intelligence Office Trump Wants appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 India’s Cockroach Movement Gains Momentum | Foreign Policy
item.description: A satirical political party has tapped into the discontent among unemployed youth.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Launches Deadly Attack on Kuwait’s Main Airport | Foreign Policy
item.description: Experts warn that continued strikes could jeopardize peace talks.

item.title: 🛡️ Experts warn terrorism threat is rising in Africa as US pulls back | Defense News
item.description: A jihadist front is gaining ground across Africa, the Center for Strategic and International Studies warned in its annual risk analysis.

item.title: 🌏 Europe has had enough of China’s export surge | Atlantic Council
item.description: Can advanced industrial democracies preserve manufacturing capacity in the face of state-backed overproduction on a continental scale? The post Europe has had enough of China’s export surge appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 New Large Chinese Submarine With Very Unique Feature Just Caught On Satellite Imagery | The War Zone
item.description: The nuclear fast attack-sized mystery submarine could be China's answer to a high-speed interceptor boat. The post New Large Chinese Submarine With Very Unique Feature Just Caught On Satellite Imagery appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🛡️ Trump strips civil-service protections from thousands of feds | Defense One
item.description: Defense, State, DHS positions among those converted to at-will jobs.

item.title: 🏴 Dispatch from Kyiv: Russia is more vulnerable than it might seem | Atlantic Council
item.description: A recent trip to Ukraine reveals a country growing in strength and confidence, while Russia is increasingly on the back foot. The post Dispatch from Kyiv: Russia is more vulnerable than it might seem appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 The seabed is becoming a battlefield. Taiwan has field notes | ASPI Strategist
item.description: As Australia strengthens the protection of critical seabed infrastructure, it should draw on Taiwan’s frontline experience. Taiwan has been battling this problem for three years. At the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore on 30 May, Deputy ...

item.title: 📍 What to Know About Ethiopia’s Election | Foreign Policy
item.description: Abiy’s all-but-certain victory threatens to destabilize the African nation.

item.title: 📍 Why Is Trump Targeting Brazilian Gangs? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. designation of two Brazilian crime groups as terrorist organizations scrambles already tense bilateral relations.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force awards GE, Rolls-Royce for ‘medium thrust’ drone engines | Breaking Defense
item.description: The two engine makers will advance designs to power medium thrust class drones, an Air Force spokesperson told Breaking Defense.
